text: Conditional on the SFT eval, do small models actually LEVERAGE the named sub-results when trained/prompted at fine granularity — i.e. do their generated proofs cite/reuse the have-steps rather than re-deriving inline — so that any pass@k gap is mechanistically attributable to USING the decomposition, not just to shorter/format-friendlier targets? Buildable by parsing generated proofs in the planned q-0007 eval logs for named-have reuse; tests whether 'granularity helped' means 'granularity was used'.
